en.extract:
The black-throated wren-babbler (Turdinus atrigularis) is a species of bird in the family Pellorneidae. It is endemic to the island of Borneo (mainly southern Brunei and East Malaysia).
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. It resides primarily in the understory. It is threatened by habitat loss. 
It is insectivorous.


References

Collar, N. J. & Robson, C. 2007. Family Timaliidae (Babblers) pp. 70–291 in; del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A. & Christie, D.A. eds. Handbook of the Birds of the World, Vol. 12. Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.

The '''black-throated wren-babbler''' ('''''Turdinus atrigularis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pellorneidae]]. It is endemic to the island of [[Borneo]] (mainly southern [[Brunei]] and [[East Malaysia]]).<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=  Phillipps, Karen |name-list-style= amp |year=2011 |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 }}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. It resides primarily in the understory.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Messina |first1=Simone |last2=Edwards |first2=David P. |last3=Eens |first3=Marcel |last4=Costantini |first4=David |date=August 2018 |title=Physiological and immunological responses of birds and mammals to forest degradation: A meta-analysis |journal=Biological Conservation |volume=224 |pages=223–229 |doi=10.1016/j.biocon.2018.06.002 |bibcode=2018BCons.224..223M |issn=0006-3207}}</ref> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]]. 

It is insectivorous.<ref name=":0" />
